instructions: Stop vehicle safely, turn on hazard lights, check for injuries, exchange details, call police if needed.
Keep seatbelt on, switch on hazard lights, do not leave vehicle unless absolutely safe, call rescue immediately.
Note fleeing vehicle number, check for injured, provide first aid, call police & rescue services.
Stop immediately, provide first aid if possible, call rescue/ambulance, do not move victim unnecessarily.
Ensure children are safe, evacuate bus if fire risk, call 1122 immediately, provide first aid.
Reduce speed, hold steering firmly, avoid sudden braking, report potholes to authorities.
At broken signals, treat as 4-way stop: first come, first go. Be cautious and patient.
Use headlights and fog lights if needed, reduce speed, maintain safe following distance.
If water above tyres, do not proceed. If shallow, move slowly in low gear and steady speed.
Reduce speed, cross diagonally if possible, avoid sudden braking.
Do not engage or race, let the driver pass, maintain safe distance, report to traffic police if possible.
Slow down, move to safe lane/side, flash headlights to warn, call police helpline.
Do not follow closely, avoid overtaking, note number plate, report immediately to police helpline.
Maintain distance, avoid overtaking in risky spots, report to traffic police helpline.
Keep safe distance, honk lightly if rider unseen, allow space, report if dangerous.
Drive at reduced speed, keep headlights/fog lights on, avoid sudden braking or large puddles.
Turn on fog lights, avoid high beam, drive slowly, keep distance, avoid overtaking.
Ensure tyres inflated properly, keep engine coolant checked, carry water for hydration.
If driving, slow down, pull over under cover if available, stay inside vehicle until safe.
Keep both hands on wheel, reduce speed, avoid overtaking, stay away from large vehicles.
Switch off engine, evacuate passengers, use fire extinguisher if small fire, call rescue services.
Shift to lower gear, pump brakes repeatedly, pull handbrake slowly, steer towards safety zone.
If water too deep, stop vehicle, move to higher ground, call rescue if stuck.
Evacuate area, do not attempt to extinguish large fire, call fire brigade & rescue.
If cracks/tilt noticed, do not cross, alert authorities, warn other drivers nearby.
Park safely, loosen nuts, use jack to lift car, replace with spare tyre.
Use jumper cables: red to +, black to -, connect to donor battery, start engine.
Turn off engine, wait 15-20 mins, open bonnet after cooling, refill coolant if available.
Switch on hazard lights, reduce speed, move to safe location.
Pull to roadside safely, switch on hazard lights, wait for emergency fuel service.
Alternate between forward and reverse gear, avoid spinning tyres too fast.
